repair_history.diagnosis:
line 13: The variable 'e' on the left side of equation is of type 'real' but must be of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl': AGREE equation variable 'e' declared as 'real' but assigned expression involves opaque types -> Change 'eq e : real' to 'eq e : Types::speed.speed_impl'
line 13: left side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type'int' or 'real': Binary operation between opaque types not supported in AGREE -> Introduce a real-valued intermediate variable for the difference
line 13: right side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type'int' or 'real': Binary operation between opaque types not supported in AGREE -> Introduce a real-valued intermediate variable for the difference
line 14: Couldn't resolve reference to 'integral'.: AGREE integral function not recognized due to type mismatch -> Replace 'integral(e)' with 'integral(real_e)' where real_e is a real-valued variable
line 15: Couldn't resolve reference to 'derivative'.: AGREE derivative function not recognized due to type mismatch -> Replace 'derivative(e)' with 'derivative(real_e)' where real_e is a real-valued variable

Couldn't resolve reference to DataSubcomponentType 'Types::speed.speed_impl'.: AADL type reference not resolvable due to missing with clause or incorrect package reference -> Replace 'Types::speed.speed_impl' with 'Base_Types::Float' to match the actual AADL type of the ports
Argument of real cast is of type '<error>' but must be of type 'int': Invalid type in real() cast due to unresolved port type -> Remove the real() cast and directly use the port expressions as they are already of compatible type
left side of binary expression '-' is of type '<error>' but must be of type'int' or 'real': Port type resolution failure causing expression type error -> Ensure port expressions are used directly without type casting
The variable 'e' on the left side of equation is of type '<error>' but must be of type 'real': Type mismatch in equation assignment due to unresolved type reference -> Change type of 'e' from 'Types::speed.speed_impl' to 'real'
Couldn't resolve reference to 'integral'.: Integral function not recognized due to type resolution issues -> Ensure integral function is used with valid real-type expressions

The variable 'real_e' on the left side of equation is of type 'real' but must be of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl': Type mismatch in equation declaration -> Change 'real_e' to 'e' in the equation declaration
left side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': Port types are not compatible for arithmetic operations -> Remove the intermediate 'real_e' variable and directly compute 'e' as 'Target - Actual'
right side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': Port types are not compatible for arithmetic operations -> Remove the intermediate 'real_e' variable and directly compute 'e' as 'Target - Actual'
Couldn't resolve reference to 'integral'.: Integral function not recognized in AGREE context -> Replace 'integral(real_e)' with 'integral(e)'
Couldn't resolve reference to 'derivative'.: Derivative function not recognized in AGREE context -> Replace 'derivative(real_e)' with 'derivative(e)'

/CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 13: right side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type'int' or 'real': Port type mismatch in subtraction operation -> Cast Target and Actual to real type using 'real(Target) - real(Actual)'

/CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 15: Couldn't resolve reference to 'derivative'.: Derivative function not recognized in AGREE context -> Replace 'derivative(e)' with 'derivative_of(e)' or use explicit derivative variable
